author: judge-correctness-c2

dimensionScores.rationale: Three old P1s resolved but the ~2000 LOC Phase-2 code shipped two NEW P1s that silently disable headline features on my lens: reconcile.py:56/65 seeds merged=scraped.copy() and skips audited-only periods, truncating FSDS deep history to yfinance's ~4y window for every scraped US large-cap; companies_house.py:197-198 digits[-1] picks the trailing yyyymmdd date not the company number, so the UK layer ingests zero rows. Plus a broad correctness tail (F8 stale import-prices, F12 single-spot FX across all periods, F13/F14 EDINET interim/parent-only, F16 BOM zeroes the GLEIF map) — net flat-to-down vs 3.7.

216 pytest + 62 vitest all green (runs/pytest.txt, vitest.txt) with the F3 PerformanceWarning gone, but the suite has demonstrable blind spots that let both P1s through: tests/test_companies_house.py:62 uses a single-digit-run fixture so digits[-1]==digits[0] and masks F7; no fixture covers an audited period outside the scraped index (F6), a co-registrant row (F10), or a BOM'd GLEIF CSV (F16). Green but false-confidence on the new sources.

Essentially flat vs 3.97. Only new issue is F5 — mirror.py:93-95 streams to disk with no byte ceiling and mirror.py:80 uses a per-op (not total) timeout, a disk-fill/slow-loris DoS on the ~200MB GLEIF auto-heal path; URLs are hardcoded/trusted (gleif.py:21, fx.py:26) so no SSRF, keyless design intact.

The F4 refactor genuinely halved service.py (820->523 LOC, verified 523) via the AuditedBulkProvider seam, but the extracted cycles all landed in enrichment.py which is now the #2 hotspot at 617 LOC nesting 14 with 9 parallel run_* cycles (F1), and mirror.py:98 writes the meta sidecar non-atomically (F2). Slight net improvement over 3.43 — complexity was relocated, not dissolved.

F3 fragmentation fixed — ranks.py:98-106 does one pd.concat and pytest shows no PerformanceWarning — but F11 introduces a fresh OOM-class regression on the self-hosted target: gleif.py:43/47 triple-buffers the whole ISIN-LEI zip (~1GB decompressed) and edgar_fsds.py reads num.txt whole into RAM, the same defect class as the prior-cycle F13 the mirror streaming was meant to prevent. Roughly flat vs 4.07.

topFindings: F6 (P1, correctness): reconcile.py:56/65 seeds merged from the scrape and skips audited-only periods, so FSDS/EDGAR deep history is truncated to yfinance's ~4y window for every scraped symbol — the headline Phase-2 deep-history feature is silently inert for US large/mid caps.

F7 (P1, correctness): companies_house.py:197-198 re.findall+digits[-1] returns the trailing yyyymmdd filing date, not the company number, on the real Prod<nnn>_<batch>_<number>_<date> filenames — the UK audited layer ingests zero rows and 'succeeds' silently; masked by the single-digit-run unit fixture at tests/test_companies_house.py:62.

Correctness tail: F16 utf-8 (not utf-8-sig) decode zeroes the whole GLEIF map on a BOM'd file; F12 applies one latest FX spot rate across all fiscal periods; F13/F14 book EDINET interim/parent-only figures as annual/consolidated; F8/F15 leave incremental compute blind to price-dump refresh and newest-raw deletion.

author: judge-pipeline-c2

dimensionScores.rationale: Old 3 P1s are genuinely fixed (shared budget service.py:442, maybe_refresh_universe:464, incremental compute snapshot.py:245), but the Phase-2 surface shipped 2 new P1 integration bugs: reconcile.py:56/65-66 seeds from scraped and skips audited-only periods so FSDS deep-history is truncated to yfinance's ~4y for every scraped US symbol, and companies_house.py:197-198 returns digits[-1]='20230331' (the date) not the company number so the UK layer ingests zero rows on real Prod<nnn>_<batch>_<number>_<date> filenames. Plus a P2 tail (F8 stale prices on the production incremental path, F9 GLEIF never-refreshed, F10 FSDS coreg, F12 FX single-spot, F13/F14 EDINET, F15/F16). Core screener intact; new audited layers partly-to-fully inert.

216 pytest + 62 vitest all green (runs/pytest.txt, runs/vitest.txt), broad breadth. But a unit fixture actively masks a P1: tests/test_companies_house.py's Prod223_1234567.html has one digit-run so digits[-1]==digits[0], hiding the real-filename parse failure (F7); and F6/F10/F16 have no representative fixture (audited period outside scraped index, co-registrant row, BOM'd CSV). Green suite, but unrepresentative fixtures let two significant bugs through.

No P0/P1 security; keyless self-hosting posture preserved. Sole item is P2 F5 (mirror.py:80/93-95): unbounded iter_bytes stream to disk and a per-op (not total) 120s httpx timeout, so a compromised upstream could disk-fill or slow-loris the ~200MB GLEIF fetch. URLs are hardcoded/trusted (GLEIF, Frankfurter) — no SSRF — and the mirror serves last-good on failure, so blast radius is low. On par with prior runs (~3.97/4.0).

The F4 refactor halved service.py (820->523, confirmed 523 LOC) but landed all 9 run_* cycles in one module: enrichment.py is now the #2 hotspot at 617 LOC, nesting 14 (F1). Seams are clean (AuditedBulkProvider, load_mapping de-dup) and per-cycle contracts tidy, but a new source keeps growing one file, and F2's non-atomic .meta sidecar write (mirror.py:89/98) is a minor robustness debt. Net flat vs the pre-fix 3.43: one file improved, one regressed, against a large new surface.

build_snapshot_incremental (snapshot.py:245) is a real O(dirty) win for 20k+ issuers via base.parquet caching. Offset by P2 F11: both bulk parsers materialize whole archives in RAM — gleif.py:43/47/49 triple-buffers the ~1GB-decompressed ISIN-LEI file and edgar_fsds.py:131-132 does archive.read().decode() on a hundreds-of-MB num.txt — OOM-killing a 1GB-RAM VPS on the auto-heal path and defeating the mirror's streaming design (same class as the prior-cycle bootstrap fix).

topFindings: F6 (P1): reconcile.py:56/65-66 skips audited-only periods, so SEC FSDS deep-history backfill is silently truncated to yfinance's ~4y window for essentially the entire scraped US large/mid-cap universe — the headline Phase-2 source is inert for its target.

F7 (P1): companies_house.py:197-198 parses the trailing yyyymmdd date instead of the company number (digits[-1]='20230331'), so the UK audited layer ingests zero rows on real Accounts Data Product filenames; the unit test's single-digit-run fixture masks it.

F8 (P2): build_snapshot_incremental derives dirty solely from data/raw stamps (snapshot.py:262), blind to prices-latest.parquet refreshes (price_import.py) — the production incremental path serves stale close/return_6m/value+momentum ranks after import-prices until a filing re-dirties the symbol.

F11 (P2): GLEIF (~1GB decompressed) and FSDS num.txt (hundreds of MB) are read whole into RAM (gleif.py:43-49, edgar_fsds.py:131-132), OOM risk on the 1GB-VPS self-hosted target, defeating the mirror's streaming design.
F1 (P2 maintainability): enrichment.py is a 617-LOC, nesting-14 hotspot holding 9 parallel run_* cycles; a new source keeps growing one file despite clean per-cycle seams.

author: judge-reliability-c2

dimensionScores.rationale: Reliability-critical prior findings verified fixed — ONE shared TokenBucket honors NFR-007 (service.py:442), the hard per-fetch watchdog is real and wired (watchdog.py + crawler.py:66, prices.py:69), stooq PoW is bounded (stooq_fetch.py:60-87) — but ~2000 lines of new audited code shipped two SILENT P1s on default/enabled paths: F6 reconcile.py:56,65-66 drops every audited period outside yfinance's ~4yr window for scraped symbols (FSDS deep-history feature inert for ~all US large caps), and F7 companies_house.py:197-198 parses the filing DATE not the company number (UK layer ingests zero rows on real filenames). The fixes offset most of the damage; the two silent, purpose-contradicting P1s + a P2 correctness cluster (F8/F9/F10/F12/F13/F14/F15/F16) hold it just below the prior 3.7.

216 pytest + 62 vitest green, no PerformanceWarning (runs/pytest.txt 3x72 dots, runs/vitest.txt) — broad on the core — but the P1s prove the NEW edge paths are undertested, and worse, F7's own unit fixture (Prod223_1234567.html, single digit-run so digits[-1]==digits[0]) actively MASKS the zero-rows bug while real Prod<nnn>_<batch>_<number>_<date> filenames fail. F6/F13/F14/F16 have no representative fixtures. Green count buys confidence the new audited layer does not earn; docked from prior 3.9 for demonstrated, bug-hiding blind spots.

No new P0/P1 security surface; bounded PoW (F12 fix) removes an untrusted-input CPU-exhaustion vector and the mirror's last-good/atomic contract fails safe. Only new item is F5 (mirror stream has no byte ceiling or total-time cap, mirror.py:80,93) — real but low: URLs are hardcoded/trusted (GLEIF, Frankfurter), no SSRF. Holds at the prior 3.97 band.

Net improvement over prior 3.43: the F4 refactor halved service.py (820->523), the AuditedBulkProvider seam and per-cycle contracts are clean, and mirror.py has a correct temp-then-rename + last-good contract (:92-96,:103-108). Offset by F1 (enrichment.py now the #2 hotspot, 617 LOC / nesting 14, 9 run_* cycles in one module) and F2 (non-atomic meta sidecar). Complexity moved rather than fully dispersed.

Incremental compute (O(dirty) via base.parquet, snapshot.py:245-274) and ETag/304 conditional mirror fetches are solid, deliberate perf wins for the 20k-issuer target; no PerformanceWarning. Docked from prior 4.07 for F11: GLEIF (~1GB decompressed, triple-buffered gleif.py:43-49) and FSDS num.txt (edgar_fsds.py:131) are read whole into RAM on the auto-heal path — an OOM-class regression on a 1GB VPS that defeats the mirror's own streaming design.

topFindings: The three reliability-critical prior findings are genuinely fixed and verified in code (shared TokenBucket service.py:442 honoring NFR-007; hard watchdog call_with_timeout wired at crawler.py:66 and prices.py:69 making the two formerly-lying docstrings true; bounded PoW stooq_fetch.py:60-87) — the CORE crawl/ingest path is materially more reliable than the 76/100 baseline.

F6 (P1, reconcile.py:56,65-66): audited-only periods are silently skipped for any yfinance-scraped symbol, so FSDS/EDGAR deep-history backfill is truncated to the ~4yr scrape window for essentially all US large/mid caps — the Phase-2 FSDS headline feature is inert for its target universe, with no error.

F7 (P1, companies_house.py:197-198): re.findall(r'\d{6,8}') + digits[-1] returns the trailing yyyymmdd DATE, not the company number, on real Accounts Data Product filenames — the entire UK audited layer ingests zero rows and reports 'success'. The unit test fixture is unrepresentative and hides it.

Test-invisibility is the through-line: 216/62 green while the UK layer ingests nothing and US deep history is truncated. Broad green coverage of the core does not extend to the new audited edge paths; one fixture actively masks a P1.

Does not clear the 80 bar (73): core hardening is real, but two silent, test-invisible P1s on default/enabled new audited layers directly contradict stated feature purpose — the new surface does not meet its own expectations. No P0; blast radius partly bounded (EDINET P2s opt-in without a key).
